item.text: I’ll locate the per-comparison renderer first, then patch the formatter and add a focused test near the existing agent-eval tests. I’ll also check the worktree state so I don’t overwrite unrelated changes.
The worktree is clean, and the relevant code is in `context-harness/src/agent_eval.rs`. I didn’t find relevant prior memory for this exact task, so I’m using the live repo as the source of truth.

I found the grouped renderer and the existing phase fields, but the named “human-debug” function isn’t obvious by a `debug` search. I’m narrowing this to every renderer/helper in the module and the CLI script that may mirror the output.

item.text: The current source no longer has a separate debug renderer, but its earlier per-comparison block renderer is visible in the file history. I’m going to restore that shape as `render_agent_eval_human_debug`, keep the grouped `render_agent_eval_human` unchanged, and add the shell phase line there with `?` for missing phase counts.

The formatter is now added as a separate debug renderer and re-exported. I’m adding the in-module unit test next, with one missing vanilla/treatment phase value so the `?` behavior is covered at the same time.
The test now asserts the new `shell_phases` line appears with `?` where phase counts are absent. I’m formatting and then I’ll run the context-harness tests through the repo’s `just test` path.

The build has reached the local `codex-network-proxy`/workspace crates, so the dependency compile is mostly past. I’m still waiting on the actual context-harness test run.
The test suite itself passed: 70 passed, 1 skipped. The `just test` recipe then automatically launched `bench-smoke`, so I’m letting that finish rather than killing the running command.

item.text:
Implemented in [agent_eval.rs](/private/var/folders/bn/jq675y6x5m1d1x83vyh99f5c0000gn/T/codex-arm-XXXXXX.5ac1eSbXaV/codex-rs/context-harness/src/agent_eval.rs:1046). The per-comparison debug renderer now emits:

`  shell_phases: discover=... read=... edit=... verify=... | <treatment> discover=... read=... edit=... verify=...`

Missing phase counts render as `?`. I also re-exported it from [lib.rs](/private/var/folders/bn/jq675y6x5m1d1x83vyh99f5c0000gn/T/codex-arm-XXXXXX.5ac1eSbXaV/codex-rs/context-harness/src/lib.rs:50) and added the unit test at [agent_eval.rs](/private/var/folders/bn/jq675y6x5m1d1x83vyh99f5c0000gn/T/codex-arm-XXXXXX.5ac1eSbXaV/codex-rs/context-harness/src/agent_eval.rs:1873).

Verification:
- `just fmt` passed, with the existing `uv` warning about `sdk/python/pyproject.toml` parsing `exclude-newer = "7 days"`.
- `just test -p codex-context-harness` passed: 70 passed, 1 skipped.
- Focused rerun passed: `just test -p codex-context-harness render_human_debug_includes_shell_phase_line`.
